text.title: ruby-rails Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ruby-rails as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Controllers call User.create(params) directly instead of using a service object.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The methodology mandates service objects. Reject this in review. Controllers are HTTP handlers, not business logic.

failure-mode.description: Agents emit before_save callbacks that mutate state in surprising ways and break tests.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Callbacks run in unpredictable order. Prefer explicit service-layer logic with one public #call method. Use callbacks only for invariants.

failure-mode.description: Sidekiq retry counts and dead: true flags are mistyped. The agent must read sidekiq.yml and existing job classes to align with team conventions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Missing retry configuration causes message loss. Inconsistent retry policies across jobs are hard to debug.

failure-mode.description: params.permit(...) is often forgotten or too permissive. Controllers pass raw params; services do not enforce allowlist.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: CI must enforce strong parameters. Mass assignment attacks otherwise possible on any field the service writes.

failure-mode.description: N+1 queries proliferate in serializers and views. Bullet catches them in dev/test only if enabled.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agent must enable Bullet before generating views/serializers. N+1 is invisible until load testing.

failure-mode.description: Rails 7's encrypted attributes need bin/rails db:encryption:init first. Agents skip this and produce migrations that crash in CI.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Encrypted attributes require setup. Review migrations before applying to non-dev environments.
